clues.clue: This prolific Menlo Park inventor is credited with the first practical incandescent light bulb.
This Scottish-born scientist won the 1876 telephone patent.
This Serbian-American visionary championed alternating-current electrical systems.
These Ohio bicycle-shop brothers achieved the first powered airplane flight in 1903.
This Swedish chemist invented dynamite and endowed a famous set of prizes.
Samuel Morse lent his name to this dot-and-dash signaling system.
Gutenberg's 15th-century machine made mass-produced books possible.
Guglielmo Marconi is credited with the first practical wireless transmission of this signal.
Ray Tomlinson sent the first networked message of this '@'-addressed type in 1971.
Tim Berners-Lee proposed this hyperlinked information system in 1989.
clues.response: What is the World Wide Web?

clues.clue: Henry Ford's assembly line cranked out millions of this affordable 'Model.'
Karl Benz patented an early gasoline-powered version of this four-wheeled vehicle in 1886.
This Scottish engineer, namesake of a power unit, vastly improved the steam engine.
Rudolf, whose surname labels a compression-ignition engine and a fuel, pioneered it in the 1890s.
Elisha Otis's safety brake made it safe for skyscrapers to lift people in this vertical car.
Inspired by plant burrs, this hook-and-loop fastener became a household brand name.
A 3M chemist's weak adhesive accidentally created this repositionable sticky note.
Gideon Sundback perfected this interlocking-teeth fastener for clothing and bags.
Laszlo Biro lent his name in many countries to this smear-resistant writing instrument.
Percy Spencer invented this kitchen appliance after a candy bar melted near his radar set.
Alexander Fleming discovered this mold-derived antibiotic in 1928.
Jonas Salk developed the first effective vaccine against this paralyzing disease.
This English doctor pioneered smallpox vaccination using cowpox.
Marie Curie won Nobel Prizes for her work on this phenomenon, a term she coined.
Wilhelm Roentgen discovered these penetrating imaging rays in 1895.

final.clue: This Apple co-founder, in a black turtleneck, unveiled the first iPhone in 2007.
